page: Alibaba Cloud ecs.sn2ne.4xlarge is a general-purpose virtual machine instance powered by the KVM hypervisor. It features 16 dedicated vCPUs mapped to 16 physical cores on an Intel Xeon E5-2682v4 processor running at 2.5 GHz. The instance is equipped with 64.0 GB of system memory, providing a balanced 4.0 GB of RAM per core. It operates on the x86_64 architecture and supports instruction sets such as AVX, AVX-512 Foundation, AES, and FMA. This instance does not include local storage or dedicated GPU hardware, relying on network-attached storage and a baseline network bandwidth of 3 Gbps.

Performance benchmarks indicate a clear distinction between single-core and multi-core capabilities. Single-core performance is relatively weak, as shown by stress-ng single-core and single-threaded gzip compression tests, which fall into the bottom tier. However, the 16 dedicated physical cores deliver average multi-core performance across stress-ng multi-core, bzip3 decompression, and PassMark CPU tests. Memory bandwidth for both small and large blocks remains in the average tier, alongside database operations and Redis SET throughput.

Given its balanced 1:4 vCPU-to-RAM ratio and dedicated CPU allocation, the ecs.sn2ne.4xlarge offers a stable resource profile for general-purpose workloads. It is well-suited for static web serving, moderate database operations, and multi-threaded data processing tasks that do not require high single-core clock speeds or local NVMe storage. From a cost-efficiency perspective, the use of older Intel Xeon hardware provides a predictable, dedicated resource pool at a lower qualitative cost tier compared to newer generation architectures, though it presents a tradeoff in single-threaded processing efficiency.
description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.sn2ne.4xlarge is a general-purpose instance featuring 16 dedicated vCPUs and 64.0 GB of RAM, powered by an Intel Xeon E5-2682v4 processor running at 2.5 GHz on the KVM hypervisor. Operating on the x86_64 architecture, it includes AVX and AVX-512 instruction support but lacks local storage and GPU accelerators. Benchmark data shows weak single-core performance, but average multi-core CPU and memory bandwidth capabilities. With a baseline network bandwidth of 3 Gbps, this instance provides a balanced 1:4 vCPU-to-RAM ratio. It is suited for multi-threaded workloads, static web serving, and moderate database operations where dedicated CPU allocation is required without the premium cost of newer hardware generations.

tagline: A dedicated x86 virtual server offering a balanced memory-to-core ratio and stable multi-threaded performance for general-purpose enterprise workloads.

Balanced 1:4 vCPU-to-RAM ratio with 4.0 GB memory per core
